content: Hey, I'm so worried about the climate crisis. I feel like I'm doing my part by carpooling to work at City Hospital, but is it enough?
That's great to hear that you're carpooling! Every little bit counts. What made you decide to start carpooling?

Well, my friend Rachel, who's also a nurse, told me about the impact of carbon emissions on air quality. We started carpooling together about 6 months ago and it's been a game-changer. We even started a podcast about sustainability on the side.
That's amazing! I'd love to hear more about your podcast. What's it called and how often do you release new episodes?

It's called 'Green Scrubs' and we release new episodes every Wednesday. We've had some great guests on, including Dr. Patel, our hospital's chief of staff. He's really passionate about reducing waste in healthcare.
I'll have to check it out. Dr. Patel sounds like a great resource. Have you considered inviting him to speak at a local event about sustainability in healthcare?

Actually, we're planning a conference at City Hospital on April 22nd, Earth Day. We're expecting around 200 attendees and we've already got a great lineup of speakers. I'm really excited to share our ideas with the community.
That sounds like a fantastic event. I'm sure it'll be a great success. Are you planning on incorporating any of your hobbies, like piano or salsa dancing, into the conference?

Ha! Well, I don't think piano will fit into the conference, but we are planning a salsa-themed fundraiser for May 1st to support our hospital's sustainability initiatives. It'll be a fun way to get people involved and raise some money for a good cause.
That sounds like a blast. I'm sure you'll get a great turnout. Have you considered partnering with local businesses to sponsor the event or donate prizes?
Yes, we're actually partnering with a few local businesses, including a sustainable clothing store and a vegan cafe. They're both donating prizes for our raffle and helping us promote the event.
That's great to hear. It's always important to build partnerships with like-minded businesses. What's the name of the vegan cafe? I'd love to check it out.
It's called 'Green Scene' and it's just down the street from the hospital. They have amazing food and a great atmosphere. I've been going there for lunch a lot lately.
I'll have to add it to my list. Thanks for the recommendation. So, what's next for your YouTube channel? You mentioned you just started it up.

Yeah, I'm really excited about it. I'm planning to post videos about sustainable living and healthcare. My first video is going to be about reducing plastic use in hospitals and it'll be up on March 15th.
That sounds like a great topic. I'm sure you'll get a lot of views. Do you have any plans for collaborations or guest videos?
Actually, I'm planning to collaborate with Rachel on a video about sustainable fashion in healthcare. We're going to film it on March 20th and it'll be up on the channel soon after.
That sounds like a great idea. I'm sure you two will make a great team. What's your goal for the number of subscribers you'd like to have in the next 6 months?
I'd love to have at least 1,000 subscribers by September. I think that's a realistic goal if we keep posting high-quality content and promoting the channel.
That's a great goal. I'm sure you'll reach it with your dedication and passion for sustainability. Keep up the good work!
Thanks for the support! I'm excited to see where this journey takes me. Maybe one day I'll even get to speak at a TED Talk about sustainability in healthcare.
I have no doubt you'll get there. You're already making a big impact with your podcast and YouTube channel. Just keep sharing your ideas and passion with the world.
Thanks, that means a lot to me. I'm looking forward to continuing this conversation and exploring more ways to make a positive impact on the environment.
Me too, Phoenix. It was great chatting with you. Let's do it again soon.
Sounds good to me. Have a great day!
You too, Phoenix. Take care!
